original_image_tags: A blank, yellowed archival page with two hole punches at the top. The page contains primarily routing stamps and a single handwritten number. Faint reverse-text bleed-through from the opposite side of the page is visible, indicating the paper is thin or the ink from the other side has saturated through.

elements.description: Back of a page featuring faint bleed-through text from the opposite side, three punched holes at the top, and several ink stamps: a rectangular stamp with inverted text reading 'COMM-EE', 'JAN 8 1968', and 'FILED 10', a handwritten red annotation, and a green stamp reading 'RECEIVED DIRECTOR' and 'JAN 22 5 25 PM '64'. No distinct photographs, sketches, or diagrams are present.

judge.reason: Both descriptions agree on the main visual content: a back page with bleed-through text, punched holes at the top, and ink stamps, but they differ on the specific number of holes (two vs. three) and the details of the stamps described.
